beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and light breakfast at home
beats.reasoning: Low energy yesterday (35/100) means a gentle start; coffee ritual and toast align with her routine. No rush given the transit strike—she walks to work anyway.

beats.activity: Walk to school downtown via familiar route
beats.reasoning: Transit strike means rideshare surge; Fatima walks most days anyway and prefers it. SLU to Downtown is ~1.5 km, manageable morning walk in mild weather.

beats.activity: Teach science classes, grade papers, prep labs
beats.reasoning: Full morning teaching block; she's productive and engaged with students (as shown in recent days). No mid-morning break needed given her introverted nature.

beats.activity: Lunch and bagel at Mighty O Donuts
beats.reasoning: Recurring lunch spot near her school; she has strong bagel opinions and frequents this place. 10 min walk from school, gives her quiet people-watching time she enjoys.

beats.activity: Return to school and finish teaching day, grade final papers
beats.reasoning: Afternoon classes and end-of-day admin work. Transit strike ends at 4pm but she walks anyway, so no schedule disruption.

beats.activity: Walk home via Pike Place Market, pick up brewing supplies
beats.reasoning: Natural detour on walk home; she was at Pike Place recently and continues her brewing project momentum. Mild weather perfect for walking and browsing.
Pike Place to SLU is ~1 km walk; 30 min accounts for leisurely pace and enjoying the clearing evening.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat simple dinner, sanitize brewing equipment
beats.reasoning: Cooking and meditative equipment prep align with her brewing passion and introverted recharge after a full teaching day. Part of her established evening ritual.

beats.activity: Sort film photos, measure grains for new batch, light reading
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening leisure combining her two hobbies—film photography and home brewing—plus reading. Low energy yesterday suggests she craves restorative solo time at home.
8.25 hours of sleep allows recovery from teaching week; ends before 6am next day as required. Reasonable bedtime for an early-rising teacher.

diary:
Started the day early with my usual routine—coffee and toast while the city was still quiet. The transit strike meant no buses, but I didn't mind the walk downtown. Something about moving through Seattle on foot, especially on a partly cloudy morning, clears my head before teaching.

Class went well. My afternoon section finally got the pH lab right, and I caught myself smiling at one student's genuine "oh!" moment when the indicator changed color. Grabbed a bagel at Mighty O—ran into another teacher in line, which broke up the day nicely.

After school, I wandered through Pike Place, picked up some new brewing supplies. The market was busy but manageable. The walk home felt earned somehow. Spent the evening sorting through old film photos—there's something about holding negatives up to the light that no digital screen can replicate—then measured out grains for a new batch. Quiet. Good.
thought_process: The strike actually worked in my favor today. I wasn't stressed about surge pricing or waiting for rideshare; I just walked, which I prefer anyway. There's something about having no choice but to move deliberately through the city that resets my mood. Teaching felt purposeful—those moments when students genuinely understand something are why I do this. I let myself linger at the market instead of rushing, which felt like a small luxury. The evening's brewing prep and photo sorting were exactly what I needed after a full day of talking and explaining. I think I'm in a good rhythm right now.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
